quotes: plasmacytoid DCs, monocytes, monocyte-DCs (having both a monocyte and DC gene signature) and a neutrophil-myeloid precursor have also recently been identified in first-trimester human skin using single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNAseq).
Unlike macrophages and LCs, very little is known about dermal DCs in developing skin beyond their presence.
summary: Review by Botting & Haniffa (2020) confirms the presence of plasmacytoid DCs in first-trimester human prenatal skin, identified by scRNA-seq. The review notes that very little is known about dermal DCs in developing skin beyond confirming their presence.
source_title: The developing immune network in human prenatal skin
snippet: Furthermore, plasmacytoid DCs, monocytes, monocyte-DCs (having both a monocyte and DC gene signature) and a neutrophil-myeloid precursor have also recently been identified in first-trimester human skin using single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNAseq).

quotes: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs) are specialized type I interferon (IFN-α/β)-producing cells.
They express intracellular TLRs TLR7 and TLR9 that mediate recognition of viral nucleic acids.
pDCs have been observed to infiltrate both murine and human skin wounds to produce type I IFNs via TLR7-and TLR9-dependent recognition of nucleic acids.
pDCs significantly secrete IL-6, an inflammatory cytokine indirectly involved in the re-epithelization of skin wounds.
summary: Review describing pDCs as specialized type I interferon producing cells that express TLR7 and TLR9. They infiltrate skin wounds and promote re-epithelialization through type I IFN and IL-6 secretion.
source_title: The regenerative potential of skin and the immune system
snippet: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs) are specialized type I interferon (IFN-α/β)-producing cells. They express intracellular TLRs TLR7 and TLR9 that mediate recognition of viral nucleic acids.

quotes: Cutaneous injury in mice drives transient TLR7- and TLR9-mediated production of type I interferon by plasmacytoid dendritic cells, which is required for re-epithelialization of the skin.
summary: Seminal paper demonstrating that pDCs sense skin injury and promote wound healing through type I interferons, mediated by TLR7 and TLR9.
source_title: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells sense skin injury and promote wound healing through type I interferons
snippet: Cutaneous injury in mice drives transient TLR7- and TLR9-mediated production of type I interferon by plasmacytoid dendritic cells, which is required for re-epithelialization of the skin.

quotes: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells have an eccentric nucleus and prominent endoplasmic reticulum and golgi (resembling a plasma cell) for the production of type I interferon.
They retain expression of the GMDP markers CD123 (IL-3R) and CD45RA
pDC have an array of surface receptors... These include the well-known human pDC markers CD303 (CLEC4C; BDCA-2), CD304 (neuropilin; BDCA-4) CD85k (ILT3) and CD85g (ILT7)
The transcription factor 4 (TCF4) is required for pDC development and for lineage identity
summary: Comprehensive review of human DC subsets including detailed phenotypic characterisation of pDCs: CD123+, CD45RA+, CD303 (CLEC4C/BDCA-2)+, CD304 (BDCA-4)+, with plasma cell-like morphology. TCF4 is the master transcription factor for pDC identity.
source_title: Human dendritic cell subsets: an update
snippet: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells have an eccentric nucleus and prominent endoplasmic reticulum and golgi (resembling a plasma cell) for the production of type I interferon... pDC have an array of surface receptors... CD303 (CLEC4C; BDCA-2), CD304 (neuropilin; BDCA-4) CD85k (ILT3) and CD85g (ILT7)

quotes: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs) were first described as interferon-producing cells
do these cells serve mainly as a major source of type I interferons or do they also make other important contributions to immune responses?
summary: Viewpoint from five leaders discussing pDC development and functions -- whether they serve solely as IFN producers or have broader immune roles.
source_title: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells: one-trick ponies or workhorses of the immune system?
snippet: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs) were first described as interferon-producing cells and, for many years, their overlapping characteristics with both lymphocytes and classical dendritic cells (cDCs) created confusion over their exact ontogeny.

quotes: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs), a BDCA-2-expressing dendritic cell (DC) subset, are generally not found in healthy human skin
they have been reported to infiltrate skin during viral infection, inflammatory diseases, or skin injuries
Cathelicidins can form complexes with DNAs and RNAs released from the dying cells, which then serve as TLR7 and TLR9 agonists to be captured by skin-infiltrated pDCs
summary: Paper on BDCA-2+ DCs in skin inflammation. Confirms pDCs are generally absent from healthy adult skin but infiltrate during injury. Key mechanism: cathelicidin-nucleic acid complexes activate pDC via TLR7/TLR9.
source_title: Re-evaluation of human BDCA-2+ DC during acute sterile skin inflammation
snippet: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs), a BDCA-2-expressing dendritic cell (DC) subset, are generally not found in healthy human skin... they have been reported to infiltrate skin during viral infection, inflammatory diseases, or skin injuries.

quotes: Immature PDCs have been found in human LN, tonsils, peripheral blood, cord blood, adult and fetal thymus, fetal liver, and fetal bone marrow.
summary: Early characterisation of BDCA-2 as a pDC-specific marker. Documents the presence of immature pDCs in fetal tissues including fetal thymus, fetal liver, and fetal bone marrow.
source_title: BDCA-2, a Novel Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cell-specific Type II C-type Lectin, Mediates Antigen Capture and Is a Potent Inhibitor of Interferon α/β Induction
snippet: Immature PDCs have been found in human LN, tonsils, peripheral blood, cord blood, adult and fetal thymus, fetal liver, and fetal bone marrow.

quotes: TCF4 as one of the master regulator transcription factors (TF) for plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDC) was found to be expressed in our dataset.
RHEX, CLEC4C, and LILRA4 were also expressed by pDC.
summary: Single-cell study identifying key pDC markers in transcriptomic data: TCF4, CLEC4C (CD303/BDCA-2), LILRA4 (ILT7), and RHEX.
source_title: Maintenance of chronic neuroinflammation in multiple sclerosis via interferon signaling and CD8 T cell-mediated cytotoxicity
snippet: TCF4 as one of the master regulator transcription factors (TF) for plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDC)... RHEX, CLEC4C, and LILRA4 were also expressed by pDC.
